Composition-root tests prove all of it without a database or a kernel: the arm is inert across three dark flag combinations, constructed with the right scope when live, tears down before shaping, and applies the plan's rates and bridges. CORRECTION ROUND 2 ITEMS 1-4 DONE (GRACE PUBLICATION SAFETY) + PG16-green + lifecycle-gate 320/320 + real API+PG contract tests + Vitest 51/51 + Playwright 9/9: (1) A POLICY WITH NO PACKAGE IS NO LONGER PUBLISHABLE. It used to report success while guaranteeing that the very next Checkout judged the configuration invalid and fell back to Emergency Grace - a UI saying 'published' over a policy that could never be used. The controlled operation rejects NULL with GRACE_PACKAGE_INVALID (PACKAGE_REQUIRED), the API answers 400/package_required, and the UI cannot even offer publication without a selection; proven at both layers, with no config row and no publication audit left behind. (2) ONE AUTHORITATIVE VALIDATOR, SHARED. iam_v2.grace_package_mismatch_reason / grace_package_matches_policy is now the SINGLE implementation of Package-Plan-typed-policy equality, called by BOTH the Hotel-Admin publication and the Checkout conversion (checkout.validateConfiguredGraceExact now delegates to it), so the two can no longer drift into the worst failure mode: publication says saved, every departure silently falls back to Emergency, and the alert queue fills with something the operator believes is configured. It proves same tenant/site, CURRENT revision of an ACTIVE package, type exactly CHECKOUT_GRACE, system-owned, NOT the reserved Emergency catalog, price zero, settlement exactly [NOT_REQUIRED], a real enabled Plan revision, duration end_mode GRACE_AFTER_CHECKOUT with the exact published seconds and the approved policy version, and EXACT equality of down/up/quota/device-limit/device-policy plus the approved VALIDITY_WINDOW accounting mode - each returned as a bounded reason. Poison tests change each scalar independently (duration, down, up, quota, device limit) and each is refused at publication with no audit row. (3) A TYPED PACKAGE SELECTOR replaces the hidden/null package field: GET /checkout-grace/packages lists only revisions publication would accept (reserved Emergency catalog explicitly excluded) with their sanitized immutable attributes, and the form PUBLISHES THE PACKAGE'S OWN PINNED VALUES read-only - the operator never pastes a UUID and never types a number, so the site config cannot contradict the pinned revision by construction. (4) OPTIMISTIC PRECONDITIONS ARE NOW MANDATORY INSIDE THE CONTROLLED OPERATIONS, not merely at the HTTP layer: publish_checkout_grace_policy requires a non-null expected_config_version >= 0 and a bounded reason; record_alert_action requires a non-null expected_state of OPEN or ACKNOWLEDGED and a bounded reason. NULL never means 'skip concurrency control' or 'act against any current state' - proven by direct SQL calls that change no version, no state and no audit row. CORRECTION ROUND 2 ITEMS 5-6 DONE (APPLIER STARTUP + INTERFACE RECONCILIATION): (5) THE STAY APPLIER IS NOW CONSTRUCTED SYNCHRONOUSLY, BEFORE ANY CONNECTOR WORK. Building it in a goroutine meant a construction failure only logged and exited while the connector kept running and kept ADMITTING EVENTS NOTHING WOULD EVER APPLY - a silently growing inbox, the exact failure this daemon exists to prevent. Startup order is now: validate flags, load the verified assignment, open the scoped repository, then synchronously build and validate the applier; a missing factory, an error, a nil applier OR A TYPED NIL (a non-nil interface holding a nil pointer, which passes an == nil check and fails only on first use) each return a bounded ErrApplierRequired, start NO connector worker, open NO PMS socket, admit NO event, and RELEASE the repository that had already been opened. Proven with a Dial spy that must stay at zero and a Close spy that must have fired, across all four failure shapes. (6) THE APPLICATION LOOPS NOW RECONCILE against the same assignment-scoped authoritative discovery the connector supervision uses, on a bounded poll (default 15s, injectable): a newly published Interface gets a loop WITHOUT a daemon restart; an Interface that stops being ACTIVE (AUTH_DISABLED / DRAINING / DECOMMISSIONED / removed) has its loop cancelled and DRAINED so no orphan worker survives; a returning Interface gets a FRESH loop rather than a resurrected one; unrelated Interfaces are never restarted or blocked (drains happen outside the loop-map lock); and the loop map is the single source of truth for whether a loop exists, so concurrent reconciliation cannot duplicate one. A discovery failure is transient by nature and keeps the existing loops applying real events rather than tearing down working application. The durable inbox remains the source of truth, so a stopped-and-restarted loop simply resumes the events still PENDING. Proven by add / remove / reactivate and a concurrent-reconcile hammer that must drain cleanly. CORRECTION ROUND 3 ITEMS 1-5 DONE (GRACE-SELECTOR EXACTNESS + HONEST RECONCILIATION EVIDENCE) + PG16-green + gate 320/320 + Vitest 51/51 + Playwright 9/9: (1) THE APPROVED POLICY VERSION IS NOW REQUIRED, NOT OPTIONAL-IF-PRESENT. A package that simply omitted duration_policy.policy_version used to pass, because 'no declared version' was being treated as 'the approved version'. It must now be PRESENT, a scalar STRING, and exactly CHECKOUT_GRACE_V1 - missing, blank, wrong, EMERGENCY_GRACE_V1, numeric, array, object and JSON null each fail with a bounded DURATION_POLICY_VERSION, proven at the direct-function, selector and publication layers plus the approved case succeeding. grace_duration_seconds is likewise required to be a real JSON number within bounds rather than merely text-equal. (2) THE WHOLE RESERVED EMERGENCY CATALOG IS EXCLUDED, not just its package code: the validator now reads the SERVICE PLAN identity too, so an ordinary-looking package that pins the reserved Emergency plan (the same reserved infrastructure through a different door) is refused with PLAN_IS_EMERGENCY_CATALOG and never offered. (3) THE SELECTOR NO LONGER MAINTAINS A SECOND, WEAKER IDEA OF PUBLISHABILITY. iam_v2.selectable_grace_packages derives each candidate's typed values from its OWN pinned immutable revision - defensively, so operator-authored JSON that is malformed excludes THAT candidate rather than failing the endpoint - and judges every one with the SAME grace_package_mismatch_reason publication uses, returning the bounded reason so an excluded package is an operator-visible diagnostic instead of a mystery or an HTTP 500. Proven: a good package is listed and publishes; poisoned end_mode, missing end_mode, non-numeric duration, oversized duration and unsupported time_accounting_mode candidates are each absent from the list while the endpoint still returns 200. (4) THE SELECTOR CARRIES THE COMPLETE SANITIZED IMMUTABLE DESCRIPTION (package code/revision, plan code/revision number, rates, quota, device limit and policy, time accounting mode, duration, end mode, policy version, settlement mode, current/active status) and the UI displays it read-only - no UUID is ever typed, no secret or guest data is exposed. (5) THE MISLEADING CONCURRENT-RECONCILE TEST IS REPLACED. Four independent supervisors each kept their OWN loop map, so that test could only ever prove they all drain - it could not prove 'exactly one loop per Interface'. The supervisor now exposes an optional loop-lifecycle observer with per-Interface GENERATIONS, and ONE production supervisor is hammered with concurrent add/remove/reactivate: peak simultaneous loops per Interface must be 1, lifecycle events must strictly alternate start/stop of the SAME generation (never overlapping), an untouched Interface must run exactly one generation throughout, a slow-draining removed loop must fully stop before its replacement starts, and shutdown must leave zero live loops. PROCESS-LEVEL duplication is separately proven where it actually lives - in the database: two processors on SEPARATE connection pools (the same relationship two daemon processes have to PostgreSQL) with eight workers each race one reservation's GI+GO, and the per-Interface advisory barrier plus the transactional claim still yield each event applied EXACTLY ONCE (last_applied_event_version = 2) with exactly one conversion audit. CORRECTION ITEM 7 DONE - PHASE-3 PHYSICAL ACCOUNTING INGESTION IS WIRED THROUGH A CONTROLLED OPERATION + PG16-green + gate 320/320: acctd's legacy loop wrote public.accounting_records, which fed neither binding attribution nor watermarks. The tick now reads each physical TC counter delta ONCE and routes it through EXACTLY ONE path: while Phase-3 accounting is live, iam_v2.ingest_accounting_sample owns the write; otherwise the legacy path runs unchanged. Writing both would double every total derived from them with no way afterwards to tell which row was the duplicate, so ownership is a decision (phase3.ownsAccounting) rather than a convention. The controlled operation keeps sampled_at (when the counter was READ) SEPARATE from the new ingested_at column (when the row arrived), because a sample taken before a Checkout boundary can legitimately arrive long after it and collapsing the two would make late usage look like it happened after the boundary; attributes each sample by the session_entitlement_bindings interval AT SAMPLE TIME rather than by the session's current pointer; advances the session counters monotonically in the SAME transaction as the sample; is IDEMPOTENT on the stable (session, sample-sequence) identity so a retry, a restart mid-tick or a duplicated delivery stores it once; reports a bounded classification (ACCEPTED / DELAYED / DUPLICATE); and FAILS CLOSED with bounded prefixes on a cross-scope session, a missing binding, a negative delta (a counter regression whose baseline is wrong) or a sample predating its own session. A refused sample is not treated as progress: acctd keeps the previous baseline so the delta is re-measured and re-offered rather than lost. Composition-root tests against real PG16 drive non-zero synthetic deltas through the same entry point the tick uses and cover: an ordinary active Entitlement (with sampled_at preserved and ingested_at distinct); two devices sharing one Entitlement (summed, not multiplied); a sample TAKEN before the boundary but INGESTED after Grace rebinding (attributed to the ORIGINAL, classified DELAYED, watermark unmoved, grace usage still zero); a sample measured after rebinding (ordinary grace usage); duplicate replay; the four fail-closed cases; a quota crossing enforced at the crossing sample's own time end to end; and flags OFF writing nothing at all. CORRECTION ITEM 8 DONE - ONE SHAPING OWNER, DECIDED AND IMPLEMENTED (ADR-0002 accepted): acctd previously held a tc client and could mutate the same kernel classes netd owns. Two writers to one piece of kernel state is a race with no owner - both reconcile at once and interleave a delete with an add; acctd re-applies a rate netd just removed, resurrecting shaping for a session that is no longer entitled; a netd re-apply wipes classes acctd believes it installed - and it degrades exactly when the appliance is busiest. DECISION: acctd owns MEASUREMENT and DERIVATION (counters, controlled accounting ingestion, true-time expiry, deriving the desired plan) and SUBMITS a COMPLETE plan to netd over its existing protected local Unix socket (POST /v1/phase3/shaping); netd, the privileged networking owner, performs ALL Phase-3 tc mutation. acctd no longer has a Phase-3 tc client at all, so it cannot race netd because it cannot write - a structural property rather than an assumption. The contract is a WHOLE PLAN, never a delta (unknown fields are rejected), because incremental instructions are how two sides drift apart. PROVEN: teardown before shaping; full idempotent reconciliation where a brand-new writer with no memory converges on the identical desired state (restart/reboot recovery is ordinary, not special); no remembered policy state on either side; a session with no addressing or no rates left UNSHAPED rather than given a silent full-speed pass; a failed teardown reported as DEGRADED with a bounded reason rather than swallowed (it means traffic may still be forwarded for ended access) while the rest of the plan still applies; concurrent submissions serialized so two half-applied plans cannot leave a state neither describes; a submission failure surfaced as degraded with bounded retry on the next tick (the plan is re-derived, so a missed submission is superseded, never stale); and zero Phase-3 query or network mutation while the flags are OFF. The offline preflight now checks the single-writer property STRUCTURALLY (13/13): netd must contain the shaping calls and acctd must contain none, so a future change that reintroduces a second writer fails the go/no-go rather than being discovered in production. ACCOUNTING ITEM 6 REOPENED AND CORRECTED (RUNTIME COMPOSITION + IDEMPOTENCY); SHAPING ITEMS 7-8 COMPLETED: the reopen was right, and the defects were real. (A) WRONG SESSION DOMAIN: the Phase-3 ingest was called from the LEGACY loop using ids from the legacy public.sessions table, while the controlled operation resolves sessions in iam_v2.sessions - EVERY call would have been refused as out-of-scope on a real appliance. The earlier tests passed because they invoked the operation directly with iam_v2 ids: they proved the OPERATION, not the COMPOSITION. There is now a dedicated Phase-3 accounting PASS that reads its own session domain (active iam_v2 sessions with their own addressing), reads each session's tc counters once, and ingests the delta; the legacy loop keeps its own domain and its own writer. (B) NON-DURABLE SAMPLE IDENTITY: the identity was an IN-MEMORY per-session counter, so after a restart it reset to 1, collided with an already-stored row, was classified DUPLICATE and the guest's real usage was SILENTLY DISCARDED with nothing anywhere saying so. The identity is now derived from the SAMPLE ITSELF (its tick instant in epoch seconds): stable across restarts, identical for a genuine retry of the same tick, monotonic, and never colliding with an unrelated counter value. (C) EVIDENCE THAT DRIVES THE WIRING: five composition-root tests now drive the PASS the tick actually calls, with synthetic tc counters - a first observation baselines and writes nothing; a real delta is attributed to the right Entitlement; a RESTART re-baselines and then measures normally with no lost and no doubled usage; the same tick replayed from a fresh arm stores exactly one sample; a re-created class (counter reset) counts from zero instead of storing a negative delta; and a dark pass reads no counters, issues no query and writes nothing. (D) TRUTHFUL DEGRADED HEALTH: netd's /v1/health now reports the Phase-3 shaping state (last applied, degraded, and the bounded problem), because a plan that failed to apply is otherwise invisible and 'shaping looks fine' is the most expensive kind of wrong; recovery clears it so a stale problem cannot linger. (E) THE SECOND WRITER IS GONE FOR REAL: when Phase-3 owns enforcement acctd's LEGACY loop now stands down entirely rather than measuring and shaping a second overlapping view of the same guests through its own tc client - which would have reintroduced exactly the race ADR-0002 removed. Then Increment 8 (the two UIs) and Increment 9 tooling.
